title: Camps * Disabilities Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Camps * Disabilities Issues 3 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues African American Community AIDS/HIV Autism Spectrum Disorder Disabilities Issues Families/Friends of People With Cancer Former Foster Children Foster Children Muscular Dystrophy Native American Community Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF EASTERSEALS OREGON EASTERSEALS OREGON PORTLAND RESIDENTIAL SUMMER CAMP AT EVANS CREEK RETREAT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers yearly 4-night summer camp retreats in two sessions, one for youth and the other for adults. The camp provides an environment that emphasizes the campers' abilities and independence, as well as the development of their social skills and appreciation and knowledge of the outdoors. Activities may include swimming, dances, talent shows, sports and games, arts and crafts, outdoor experiences, and nature hikes. Provides all meals and snacks. 237 NE Broadway Street Suite 100 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Ages 12 and older with a diagnosed disability Hours: Varies Email (503) 228-5100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERSEALS OREGON EASTERSEALS OREGON PORTLAND OREGON SUMMER DAY CAMP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides day camps with safe, fun, and engaging activities including arts and crafts, nature and exploration, sports and recreation, and performing arts. Campers must provide their own sack lunch. 237 NE Broadway Street Suite 100 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Ages 10-14 with a diagnosed disability Hours: Camp sessions: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Office: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5pm, Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (503) 222-8500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EPILEPSY FOUNDATION OREGON CAMP DISCOVERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a summer camp where children experience archery, rock climbing, hiking, swimming, arts/crafts, campfire games and much more. Families can rest assured that their loved ones will be under constant medical supervision 24 hours a day while at camp. 16409 SE Division Street Suite 216 Pmb 1115 Portland, OR 97236 Eligibility: Children with epilepsy and their siblings ages 7-16. Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm. Camp: July 26-29, 2026. Registration opens April 2026. Email (503) 205-1404 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEART OF OREGON CORPS GO! LEAD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers employment opportunities, job training, and environmental education for youth interested in Camp LEAD or have attended in the past. Participants are paid to help maintain public recreation areas and learn about outdoor related careers. Employment takes place at Camp LEAD, and participants have opportunities to engage in activities, play games, hone camp skills, and make friends. 1291 NE 5th Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 experiencing a disability Hours: Varies Email (541) 633-7834 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERSEALS OREGON RESIDENTIAL SUMMER CAMP AT EVANS CREEK RETREAT EPILEPSY FOUNDATION OREGON CAMP DISCOVERY HEART OF OREGON CORPS GO! LEAD
